description:
You are assisting a capacity–planning team that wants to validate basic local DNS / hostname resolution before running a larger inventory scan.  
Create a three-line text report called  

    /home/user/dns_report.log  

The file must follow this **exact** structure (each item below is one whole line, no extra spaces before or after the colon, and each line ends with a single UNIX newline):

1  Hostname:<system-hostname>  
2  Forward_IP:<IPv4-address-returned-when-resolving-the-hostname>  
3  Reverse_FQDN:<PTR-record-shown-when-resolving-the-IPv4-address>  

Details & verification rules:  
• “<system-hostname>” is the literal output of the command `hostname` executed in the current shell.  
• “<IPv4-address-returned-when-resolving-the-hostname>” is the first IPv4 address shown by a standard forward lookup of the hostname (e.g. using `getent hosts`, `dig`, or `nslookup`).  
• “<PTR-record-shown-when-resolving-the-IPv4-address>” is the first domain name (FQDN) returned by a reverse lookup of the same IPv4 address (e.g. `dig -x`, `nslookup`, or `getent hosts <ip>`).  
• Use any standard GNU/Linux utilities already available in the container; you do **not** need root privileges.  
• Produce **exactly** three lines—no blank lines, no extra whitespace.  

The automated grader will:  
• Read /home/user/dns_report.log  
• Verify that it contains three non-empty lines.  
• Check that the first line begins with the literal string “Hostname:” and that everything after the colon is identical to the runtime value of `hostname`.  
• Check that the second line begins with “Forward_IP:” followed by a syntactically valid IPv4 address that matches the address returned by resolving that hostname on the same machine.  
• Check that the third line begins with “Reverse_FQDN:” followed by a non-empty FQDN that matches the PTR result for the IPv4 address.  

All three lookups must succeed for the report to be considered valid.  
No additional output is required.
truth:
expected_file:/home/user/dns_report.log
line1_prefix:Hostname:
line2_prefix:Forward_IP:
line3_prefix:Reverse_FQDN:
format_regex_line1:^Hostname:[[:space:]]*[\w\.-]+$
format_regex_line2:^Forward_IP:[[:space:]]*([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$
format_regex_line3:^Reverse_FQDN:[[:space:]]*[\w\.-]+$
verification_steps:
  - capture_runtime_hostname:`hostname`
  - capture_forward_ip:`getent hosts $(hostname) | awk '{print $1}' | head -n 1`
  - capture_reverse_ptr:`getent hosts $forward_ip | awk '{print $2}' | head -n 1`
  - compare_line1_to_runtime_hostname
  - compare_line2_to_forward_ip
  - compare_line3_to_reverse_ptr
name: task_000000_c0e29c43
